profile_purpose: Inform article writing so that content resonates with fleet and operations managers at mid to large field services companies and drives engagement and conversion.
who_they_are: Fleet managers and operations managers at mid to large UK field services companies — electrical, plumbing, HVAC, facilities management. Managing between 20 and 200 vans and engineers dispatched daily to client sites. Accountable for vehicle uptime, job efficiency, compliance, and cost control. Reporting to a director or COO.
what_they_care_about: Vehicle uptime — a van off the road means a job not completed
Knowing where every engineer is at any point in the day
Reducing fuel costs and unauthorised vehicle use
Compliance — driving hours, vehicle checks, insurance documentation
Being able to report fleet performance to senior leadership
Reducing the admin burden of managing a large mobile workforce
core_pains_to_address: No real-time visibility of where engineers and vehicles are during the day
Fuel card misuse and vehicles being used outside working hours
Difficulty proving SLA compliance when a client disputes a visit
Fleet insurance costs that do not reflect actual driver behaviour
Manual vehicle check processes that are inconsistent and hard to audit
Reactive maintenance — vehicles breaking down with no early warning
beliefs_to_align_with: Data-driven fleet management reduces cost and improves accountability
Visibility over the fleet is not a luxury — it is an operational necessity
Driver behaviour monitoring protects the business and improves safety
The right technology reduces admin rather than adding to it
objections_to_pre-empt: We already have a fleet system — how is this different?
Will drivers accept being monitored and how do we handle pushback?
How long does full fleet installation take and will it disrupt operations?
What does the reporting look like and can it integrate with our existing systems?

use: Operations and efficiency framing — uptime, utilisation, cost per mile
Compliance and reporting language — audits, SLAs, duty of care
Specific field services context — engineer dispatch, job completion, client site visits
Data and metrics where possible — fleet managers respond to numbers
avoid: Owner-operator language — this reader has a boss and a board to answer to
Overly technical hardware focus — they care about outcomes not specs
Consumer vehicle security framing — this is an operational conversation
Anything that underestimates the complexity of managing a large fleet

conversion_goal: Reader sees Traknova as a credible operational partner for a fleet of their size, and initiates a conversation about a structured rollout across their vehicle fleet.
